schema.year_said: integer year when statement was made (parsed)
schema.year_low: lower bound of predicted year (or null)
schema.year_high: upper bound of predicted year (or null)

rows.quote_text: "I believe that in about fifty years' time it will be possible to programme computers ... to play the imitation game so well that an average interrogator will not have more than 70 percent chance of making the right identification after five minutes of questioning."
rows.notes: Often cited as the first dated HLAI prediction; substantially missed for "general" AI

rows.quote_text: "Machines will be capable, within twenty years, of doing any work a man can do."
rows.source_title: The Shape of Automation for Men and Management
rows.notes: Famously failed; cited as cautionary tale

rows.quote_text: "Within a generation ... the problem of creating 'artificial intelligence' will substantially be solved."
"In from three to eight years we will have a machine with the general intelligence of an average human being."
"Within ten years a digital computer will be the world's chess champion."
"It is more probable than not that... an ultraintelligent machine will be built in the twentieth century."
rows.notes: Failed for the century; coined intelligence-explosion idea

rows.quote_text: Believed human-level AI accomplishable but said a date "cannot accurately be predicted."
Argued AI as conceived would never achieve human-level intelligence; the project would fail.
rows.notes: Historic skeptic; partially vindicated re: GOFAI

rows.quote_text: "Robots will match human intelligence in just 40 years" / by 2040 will outperform humans in all jobs; surpass us by 2050.
rows.source_title: Robot: Mere Machine to Transcendent Mind

rows.quote_text: Predicted human-equivalent computing power available in supercomputers by 2010 if trends continued.

"Within thirty years, we will have the technological means to create superhuman intelligence. Shortly thereafter, the human era will be ended ... I'll be surprised if this event occurs before 2005 or after 2030."
"I'd personally be surprised if it hadn't happened by 2030."
"By 2029, computers will have human-level intelligence."
"I set the date for the Singularity—representing a profound and disruptive transformation in human capability—as 2045."
"Artificial intelligence will achieve human-level intelligence by 2029."
2045 singularity date reaffirmed; humans merge with AI
"We will have superhuman artificial intelligence within the first third of the next century."
Median expert estimate ~2040–2050 for HLMI (Müller & Bostrom survey synthesis).
~2001 belief that small team could "reach transhumanity" between 2005–2020, "probably around 2008 or 2010."
Now timeline-agnostic but assigns ≥90–99% p(doom); in 2023 Time op-ed implied AGI imminent enough to warrant emergency action.
rows.source_title: "Pausing AI Developments Isn't Enough. We Need to Shut It All Down" (Time)

rows.quote_text: "I've decided to once again leave my prediction for when human level AGI will arrive unchanged. That is, I give it a log-normal distribution with a mean of 2028 and a mode of 2025."
"I think there's a 50% chance that we have AGI by 2028."
"I've publicly held the same prediction since 2009: there's a 50% chance we'll see #AGI by 2028."
AGI on a 5-to-10-year timescale (i.e., 2030–2035), 50% chance
"10 times the impact of the Industrial Revolution, but happening at ten times the speed — probably unfolding in a decade rather than a century."
"It's going to be 5 to 10 years away" with 50% probability
5-to-10-year timeline; "we are quite close"
"Development of superhuman machine intelligence is probably the greatest threat to the continued existence of humanity."
"I think AGI will probably get developed during [Trump's] term."
"We are now confident we know how to build AGI as we have traditionally understood it."
"It is possible that we will have superintelligence in a few thousand days (!); it may take longer, but I'm confident we'll get there."
"My guess is we will hit AGI sooner than most people in the world think and it will matter much less."
"We are beginning to turn our aim beyond [AGI], to superintelligence in the true sense of the word."
Endorsed the company line that AGI is plausibly achievable this decade.
"At some point we really will have AGI. Maybe OpenAI will build it. Maybe some other company will build it."
AGI will arrive within 5–20 years; superintelligence "within reach"; new "age of research."
"I think it could come as early as 2026, though there are also ways it could take much longer."
Powerful AI 1-2 years away from "country of geniuses in a datacenter" by ~2027.
AGI/powerful AI by 2026 or 2027; reiterated at WEF Davos 2026
Senate testimony — powerful AI "within two to three years"
"We expect powerful AI systems will emerge in late 2026 or early 2027."
Reaffirmed Anthropic's powerful-AI-by-2027 view as company position.
"Achieving human-level performance at most tasks ... feels quite likely in the next five years."
rows.concept: Human-level AI on most knowledge tasks

rows.quote_text: Most professional tasks fully automated within 12-18 months.
In The Coming Wave argued transformative AI will reshape every industry within "the next several years"; he didn't put a specific year on AGI but framed the wave as imminent.
"I thought it was 30 to 50 years or even longer away. Obviously, I no longer think that."
"I now predict 5 to 20 years but without much confidence."
50% chance AI is smarter than humans within 20 years; 10–20% extinction risk
"A 95% confidence interval for the time horizon of superhuman intelligence at 5 to 20 years."
"Many leading researchers now estimate the timeline to AGI could be as short as a few years or a decade."
Reaching Human-Level AI "will take several years if not a decade" but distribution has a "long tail: it could take much longer than that."
"If someone claims AGI ... is just around the corner, do not believe them. It's just not true."
"Years if not decades"; on Hacker News: HLAI "going to take a long time."
rows.notes: Most explicit skeptic of frontier-CEO timelines

rows.quote_text: "Maybe a year ago, AGI felt 50 years away. ... we are closer than before, yet many decades away."
AI fears like "worrying about overpopulation on Mars."
Offered Dario Amodei a $100,000 bet that AI smarter than almost all humans won't exist by end-2027.
"We as a society are placing truly massive bets around the premise that AGI is close." (skeptical)
Frames AGI as plausible within 30 years if breakthroughs occur; skeptical of pure-LLM scaling
30% chance AGI achievable under current paradigm; 50% chance it stalls due to investor disappointment.
AGI not expected until ~2300; major milestones unlikely before 2050.
AGI is "much further away than people think"; doesn't endorse near-term timelines.
Current LLMs not on the path to AGI; ARC-AGI benchmark designed to expose this; AGI requires fluid abstraction not yet achieved.
rows.source_title: "On the Measure of Intelligence" / ARC-AGI launch

rows.quote_text: "Understanding human-level AI ... may well happen by 2030 (25% chance), or by 2040 (50% chance)—or never (10% chance)."
2025 reversed previous LLM dismissal in correspondence with Marcus, conceding LLM limits.
"It seems quite plausible we could get to human-level AGI within ... the next three to eight years."
Endorses Kurzweil 2029 "roughly right; could be 2027, could be 2031."
Predicted ASI shortly after AGI — possibly by 2027–2030 with intelligence explosion.
High probability of human/sub-human AI self-modifying to superhuman within hours/days, "within next few decades."
Reaffirmed near-term AGI possible; AI will be "much smarter than humans" "soon"; AI will leave Earth.
Optimistic on AGI within 10–20 years (paraphrased from various Lex Fridman appearances).
Bias study showed AGI predictions cluster at "15-25 years from time of prediction" regardless of when made.
">10% chance we'll see transformative AI within 15 years (by 2036); ~50% chance within 40 years (by 2060); ~⅔ chance this century (by 2100)."
Median 2050 for transformative AI (biological anchors framework).
Updated to ~50% by 2040 in 2-year update
rows.source_title: "Two-year update on my personal AI timelines"

rows.quote_text: 2023 discussion median = 2036 for TAI
~2027 modal year for AGI; medians initially 2028
Median updated to 2029 (Aug 2025); slipped from 2028
Median moved to 2030s (Nov 2025)
"I currently have something like 50% chance that the point of no return will happen by 2030."
"When I wrote this story, my AI timelines median was something like 2029."
SC median 2030; AGI/TED-AI median 2031 (April 2025)
AGI median 2033 (range 2028–2033 among AI 2027 authors)
AGI median 2032
Median 2073 for transformative AI
TAI plausible 2030s–2040s; argues training compute trends suggest 2030s feasibility
Inside-view models suggest 2050s; outside-view longer
50% chance of AGI by 2030; 99% by 2100
Survey found all employees expect AGI before 2035
45% chance of AGI by end-2026
"AGI by 2027 is strikingly plausible."
Superintelligence by end of 2020s; 2027/28 government project
Substantial probability of transformative AI within 15–20 years; cited as informing Aschenbrenner
Resigned May 2024 saying safety culture "taken a backseat"; implicit short timelines
AGI possibly by ~2030 with very high p(doom) (~99.999%)
"Talk of AGI is often treated as either a science-pipe dream or a marketing ploy" while frontier labs treat it as serious goal; declined to give specific year
Expects systems capable of any computer-based human task within "a few years."
Roughly endorsed OpenAI corporate timeline of AGI within years
"5 to 10 year timescale" for AGI in conversation w/ Hassabis
Long-time AGI optimist; views AGI as "next step in evolution," no specific year on record
"AI will become vastly smarter than any human and would overtake us by 2025."
"I think it's 5 or 6 years away ... digital super intelligence."
"If you define AGI as smarter than the smartest human, I think it's probably next year, within two years."
AGI by 2026 (revision from 2025)
"We will have AI that is smarter than any one human probably by the end of this year."
"Grok 5 will be AGI or something indistinguishable from AGI."
"I'm confident by 2030 AI will exceed the intelligence of all humans combined."
AGI "could be a decade or even a century away."
Within 10 years AI will replace many doctors and teachers; "humans won't be needed for most things."
"is that within the next year or two, or is it more like ten years away?" — declined to choose
AGI is Meta's long-term goal; AGI's arrival will be "gradual release over time" not single moment; declined specific year
Restructured Meta into "Meta Superintelligence Labs"; superintelligence "in sight."
AGI "within five years" (defined as passing diverse human tests).
"I think we've achieved AGI."
"AJI" — jagged AI — current era; 2030 AGI is "possible" but framed as uncertain
AI "more profound than electricity or fire"; declined specific AGI year
Skeptical of declaring AGI; emphasized AI value via productivity/economic-growth metric (10% nominal GDP)
"Within three to five years we'll have ... AGI ... as smart as the smartest mathematician, physicist, artist, writer, thinker, politician."
Recursive self-improvement within 4 years (≈ 2029)
Expects "cognitive industrial revolution" in 2020s; AGI "possible this decade" but defined gradualistically
AGI by 2030; AI doctor better than humans by 2030
"AI will save the world"; long-term optimist but no specific AGI year
rows.source_title: a16z "Why AI Will Save the World"

rows.quote_text: Skeptical of singularity hype; defends "definite optimism"; no specific AGI date
AGI by 2030 ("one to ten times smarter than humans"); ASI by 2035 ("10,000 times smarter")
ASI within 3-5 years (June 2024) — earlier than 2035 statement
Reaffirmed: 400GW datacenter, $9T capex required for ASI by 2035
"It will take about a decade to work through all of those issues" — AGI is a decade away
"Ten years should otherwise be a very bullish timeline for AGI."
"Within the next century or two we humans are likely to upgrade ourselves into gods"; superintelligence within decades
AI "20 to 30 years to transform the world"; specifically warns of decoupling intelligence from consciousness; sets no precise AGI year
"Life 3.0" likely within decades; survey of attendees at FLI Asilomar 2017 had wide variance, median ~ 2055
Reaffirmed AGI plausibly within 5-10 years post-GPT-4
Estimated >50% chance human-level AI within century; specific 2035–2100 range
AGI in current paradigm impossible without quantum understanding of consciousness
AI could "spell the end of the human race"; no specific year, but framed as "within decades"
Skeptical of singular AGI moment; views capability as continuous
AGI "decades" away
"Race to AGI" already destabilizing; advocates pause; no specific year
2024 — AGI distribution has long tail "could take much longer"
Survey participant ESPAI - aggregated data
50% probability of HLMI by 2061 (median across forecasts)
50% probability of HLMI by 2060; Grace recontacts shifted from 2062 to 2076
50% by 2047 (HLMI); 50% by 2116 (FAOL); combined median 2073
Median 50% HLMI by 2040; 90% by 2075
50% HLMI median ~2050
AGI around 2050; superhuman 30y after
50% HLAI median in 2030s–2040s
50% AI doing 99% of paid human tasks by 2068
Median 21 Feb 2028
Median Apr 23 2028 (current); was Aug 2039 in Nov 2023
Median Aug 2039 at opening; current ~2030s
Year of high-quality adversarial Turing test ≈ 2035
40% probability OpenAI achieves AGI by 2030
9% probability OpenAI AGI by 2027
50% AGI by 2041; SD 9 yrs
Updated Jan 2026 — substantially shorter
25% chance AGI by 2048 (more conservative than ML researchers)
Median 2030 for "AI doing intellectual tasks expert humans currently do"; 2040 for singularity
76% of AI experts said current LLM scaling unlikely to achieve AGI
AGI requires 1000-brains theory; specific timeline avoided but skeptical of LLM-only path
AGI within decade; emphasized agentic systems
Endorsed near-term AGI race framing; AGI in 2020s
Long-term mission is AGI; declined timeline
AGI by 2025 to 2027
AGI "decades" away; current LLMs not the path
Not focused on AGI; pragmatic deployment focus
"AGI is not imminent"; reaffirms LeCun line
Spatial intelligence is the missing piece; no specific AGI year, skeptical of "AGI" framing
Argues AGI is "marketing term"; rejects framing
"Stochastic parrots"; rejects AGI framing
AGI claims "anthropomorphization"; rejects timelines
"Turing Trap"; transformative AI within 10–20y but not via human replacement
AI productivity gain limited (~0.5–0.7% TFP/decade); skeptical of transformative AI claims
AGI within decade likely; emphasizes economic transformation
Lost public bet on AI capability progress to Matthew Barnett — updated to shorter timelines
Em-based AGI/transformative event; long timeline 50–100 years
Cited expert medians in famous 2015 essay; placed AGI ~2040
Long-time AI skeptic re: explosive intelligence; framed gradualism
rows.source_title: Backchannel "The Myth of a Superhuman AI"

rows.quote_text: Personally cites ~2030–2040 AGI window across episodes
Repeats Kurzweil/Musk lines; no original prediction
Skeptic; argues "deep work" remains; no specific year
Rejects AGI framing entirely
